content: Yes, the timing of a safety car helped Kimi Antonelli to Sunday's win at the Japanese GP, but he was still the fastest car on track when it happened.

content: The Consulate General of Iran in Mumbai stated on Monday that there have been no direct talks with the US, only excessive and unreasonable demands passed through intermediaries.

content: Actor-turned-politician Vijay found himself consoling his party's Villivakkam candidate Sabarinathan, who broke down on stage as his name was announced

title: Top Karnataka GST Officer Busted While Allegedly Taking Bribe Of Rs 6 Lakh
content: It is alleged that the 48-year-old accused officer demanded a bribe of Rs 10 lakh to settle the matter, which was later negotiated down to Rs 6 lakh.

title: Manufacturing Drives India's Industrial Output Growth To 5.2% In February
content: The National Statistics Office (NSO) revised the industrial production growth for January 2026 to 5.1 per cent from the provisional estimate of 4.8 per cent released earlier this month.

content: This is coercive bargaining under continuing fire. Every side is trying to improve its position before the next move.

content: Now, it is not just Iran's nuclear programme that will be a threat to the US and the world. It will likely release a nuclear genie that will be hard to put back in the bottle.

content: Trump has outlined plans for the new White House ballroom, saying the project will address space constraints during official events and feature bulletproof and drone-resistant design elements.

content: With the inauguration of Noida International Airport, years of anticipation along the Yamuna Expressway have turned into a visible, operating reality.

content: Aniruddhacharya's counsel said viral video clips would harm his image. "I will not be taken seriously with what is circulated."

content: US Israel Iran War News LIVE Updates: The United States and Israel continue to strike Iran, whose retaliatory attacks have targeted Israel and neighboring Gulf Arab states. More than 3,000 people have been killed.

title: Nifty50 ends below 22,350; Sensex plunges over 1,600 points - top reasons for fall
content: Stock market crash today: Nifty50 and BSE Sensex continued their slide on Monday, dropping sharply on negative global cues. While Nifty50 went below 22,350, BSE Sensex closed below 72,000.

title: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi: Can the ‘bindaas’ star avoid second-season blues?
content: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi took the Indian Premier League (IPL) by storm last season, smashing 24 sixes off just 122 balls. In seven matches, he piled up 252 runs at a staggering strike rate of 206.55. Since then, he has continued his explosive form and helped India U-19 win the title. The second-season blues have affected many young stars in the past; the big question remains: will Vaibhav be able to sustain his meteoric rise?

title: As drones change warfare, Pentagon is taking 'Amazon's help' to solve an 'old problem'
content: The US Army is launching a new online marketplace for drones. Amazon Web Services is assisting in its development. This platform aims to speed up procurement for soldiers and allies. It will offer a single storefront for comparing and ordering drone systems. The initiative seeks to modernize weapons acquisition and foster competition.

title: Dhurandhar: The Revenge box office collection day 12: Film eyes Rs 1000 crore
content: 'Dhurandhar 2: The Revenge' has officially made history with its day 11 collections. The film enters its second Monday and day 12, today,  March 30, 2026. In a stunning display of box office dominance, the Ranveer Singh starrer has surpassed the lifetime India net of its predecessor, 'Dhurandhar Part 1' with its Rs. 840.20 crore, in just 11 days - a feat that originally took 15 weeks. With a current domestic net of Rs. 848.55 crore and a global gross of Rs. 1,361.95 crore, the Aditya Dhar directorial is now chasing the elite Rs. 1,000 crore India net club.

title: 'Beginning of whistle revolution': TVK chief Vijay launches assembly poll campaign in Chennai
content: TVK president Vijay commenced his election campaign from Perambur in Chennai. He criticized Chief Minister M K Stalin regarding an increase in crime and a deteriorating law and order situation. Vijay stated that the current administration has failed to ensure basic safety for citizens. He urged voters to support the TVK's whistle symbol, calling the election a 'whistle revolution'.

title: 'Modi controls Kerala CM': Rahul counterattacks Vijayan with same BJP 'B-team' barb
content: Kerala's political landscape is dominated by accusations of being the "BJP B team." Rahul Gandhi claims the LDF is BJP-backed, while CM Pinarayi Vijayan labels the UDF as the "BJP B team." Rahul criticizes the LDF as a corporate-funded government, alleging BJP control over Kerala's leadership and highlighting the Sabarimala gold theft.

title: Google founder to employees at town hall: This year a big role will be played by AI agents
content: Google employees are buzzing about Agent Smith, an internal AI tool automating tasks and accessible via mobile. Its popularity led to restricted access, highlighting Google's focus on AI agents for productivity in 2026, as emphasized by cofounder Sergey Brin. AI adoption is becoming mandatory across the company, with usage even impacting performance reviews.

title: 5 IPL records that look impossible to break, but not for Vaibhav Sooryavanshi
content: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi has been on a record-breaking/setting spree over the last year or so and, as he prepares for his second IPL season, expectations are sky-high from the 15-year-old boy wonder.  So ,what can we expect from Sooryavanshi in his second season? A lot - plus these five iconic IPL records, which were once thought unbeatable, could very well fall to the 15-year-old teenager.

title: Microsoft AI CEO reveals what will really decide AI's winners and losers by 2028
content: Microsoft AI CEO Mustafa Suleyman asserts that the AI industry's future hinges on who can afford to run models at scale, not just who builds the smartest ones.  He argues that inference compute scarcity will define winners for the next few years, with high-margin products gaining a significant edge through a data-driven improvement flywheel.

title: Dual-airport strategy in Delhi, Mumbai signals India's aviation boom
content: India's aviation landscape is undergoing a significant transformation, shifting from single, overburdened airports to a dual-airport model in major cities like Delhi-NCR and Mumbai. This strategic move aims to alleviate congestion, foster economic growth, and position India as a global aviation hub, mirroring international standards and preparing for a future of mass air travel.

title: SSC CPO result 2026: Delhi Police SI cut-off, PET/PST list out
content: SSC CPO Result 2026: The Staff Selection Commission (SSC) has declared the SSC CPO 2025 Paper-I result for Sub-Inspector posts in Delhi Police and CAPFs. Candidates shortlisted for PET/PST have been listed category-wise with cut-off marks for female, male, and Delhi Police departmental candidates. The exam was held in December 2025, and results are based on normalized scores. Shortlisted candidates will now appear for physical tests, with schedules to be released soon on the official SSC website.

title: NFL Trade Rumors: Dolphins, Chargers linked to $24mn 49ers star
content: NFL trade rumors are gaining momentum again as Jauan Jennings is attracting significant trade interest from the Miami Dolphins, Los Angeles Chargers, and Titans, with the 49ers having moved on. Jennings offers a valuable skill set, excelling in red zone situations and contested catches, making him a target for teams seeking dependable receiving options. As of now, Jauan Jennings ' situation is very clear. San Francisco has moved on, and three teams have shown interest. The next step now depends on who decides to act first.

title: Aaron Rodgers Steelers: TJ Watt’s $123mn deal 'not even close'
content: The Pittsburgh Steelers face a significant financial challenge in the 2026 offseason. With T.J. Watt's hefty contract and Aaron Rodgers seeking a substantial raise, the Steelers' cap space is severely strained. Watt's production has declined, raising questions about his value, while Rodgers' strong performance has inflated his market, forcing a tough decision on which star to prioritise. Now, they must choose. Stick with a high-priced defence led by Watt or adjust the books to bring Rodgers back. Either way, the margin for error is gone.

title: 'BJP deceived people of Bihar': Tejashwi Yadav on CM Nitish Kumar's resignation as MLC
content: Rashtriya Janata Dal leader Tejashwi Yadav accused the BJP of deceiving Nitish Kumar and the people of Bihar by removing him as Chief Minister. This comes as Nitish Kumar and BJP's Nitin Nabin resigned from their state legislature seats after being elected to the Rajya Sabha, a mandatory step.

title: Cameron Green workload row: Who calls the shots, KKR or Cricket Australia?
content: Cameron Green's massive INR 25.20 crore IPL price tag is under scrutiny as he's unavailable to bowl due to a lower back injury. Cricket Australia clarified KKR knew of the situation, sparking debate about player workload management and franchise rights versus national board control. This situation has left KKR struggling with team balance.

title: DIY decor: Art expert reveals budget-friendly home projects going viral
content: Art expert spills the beans on 5 budget-friendly DIY decor trends that are a rage this year, focusing on budget-friendly, photogenic and easily replicable projects. Peel-and-stick transformations, personalised wall art, colour-led hacks, upcycled vintage furniture and small-space glow-ups are highlighted as key trends. These accessible projects empower anyone to creatively transform their living spaces.

title: Rupee hits record low: Currency falls past 95 against US dollar
content: The Indian rupee saw a significant rebound, gaining 128 paise to 93.57 against the US dollar following RBI's directive to limit banks' currency exposure. This move prompts banks to reduce dollar holdings, offering temporary support.

title: Visakhapatnam horror: Navy staffer kills woman, dismembers body, stores remains in fridge
content: A shocking incident unfolded in Vizag as a Naval technician allegedly murdered his girlfriend, dismembered her body, and disposed of some parts. The accused surrendered to police after confessing to the brutal crime. Investigators recovered some body parts from his refrigerator, but the head remains missing, prompting a dedicated search.

title: 'KKR fully aware': Cricket Australia forced to clarify on Green after Rahane's 'ask CA' jibe
content: Cricket Australia clarified Cameron Green's inability to bowl for Kolkata Knight Riders in IPL 2026, citing a lower back injury. This came after KKR skipper Ajinkya Rahane's pointed remarks about the Australian board. Green is undergoing managed rehabilitation and is expected to resume bowling in 10-12 days, with KKR fully informed.

title: Prakash Raj's mother passes away at the age of 86
content: Veteran actor Prakash Raj's mother, Swarnalatha, has passed away at the age of 86. She died at the actor's residence after a brief illness. Swarnalatha was a strong support in Prakash Raj's life and career. The film industry mourns this personal loss. Many actors and filmmakers have offered their condolences. Her final rites were held in Bengaluru.

title: 'Dubai could be blown up': US economic expert flags risks for UAE if it enters Iran war
content: Top US economist Jeffrey Sachs warned the UAE that its alignment with the US and Israel could expose Dubai and Abu Dhabi to serious threats if it enters the ongoing Iran war. He criticized the Abraham Accords as an "invitation for disaster," arguing that overreliance on American security guarantees is a dangerous miscalculation for the Gulf nation.

title: Lok Sabha passes Bill to amend Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code; here's what it means
content: The Lok Sabha has approved the Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code (Amendment) Bill, 2025, a move lauded by Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman for revitalizing India's banking sector. The amendments aim to expedite case admissions and curb system misuse, building on the IBC's success in resolving over half of stressed assets and improving corporate governance for rescued firms.

title: Water discovery fuels return to Moon
content: Four astronauts are set to launch on a historic lunar mission, marking humanity's return to the Moon after over 50 years. This revival of crewed lunar exploration was significantly fueled by the discovery of water by India's Chandrayaan-1, which reignited global interest and provided crucial scientific justification for future missions.

title: Less shower time, no elevators & more: How Iran war is seeping into daily life worldwide
content: The Middle East conflict's global impact is escalating, disrupting supply chains and raising costs worldwide. From Australian farmers facing fertilizer shortages to South Koreans cutting shower times, nations are implementing energy-saving measures. This crisis, fueled by disruptions in vital shipping routes like the Strait of Hormuz, is reshaping daily lives and economies across continents, forcing significant adjustments.

title: 'Umpires ne bola side ho jaayein': First-hand account of PSL ball-tampering controversy
content: Pakistan Super League (PSL) is embroiled in a ball-tampering scandal after a controversial incident involving Lahore Qalandars players. Umpires awarded five penalty runs to Karachi Kings after deeming the ball deliberately altered, leading to a reduced target and a Lahore Qalandars loss. Opener Fakhar Zaman has denied charges of ball-tampering.

title: Petrol, diesel price: Global crude oil costs rise; what's the situation in India?
content: Last week, the government announced a big excise duty cut on petrol and diesel prices, in effect cushioning consumers from a hike, while also reducing the blow for oil marketing companies.

title: ‘Rohit 2.0 will worry all IPL teams’: Kumble hails MI star’s blazing return
content: Anil Kumble lauded Rohit Sharma's explosive 78 off 38 balls, heralding a formidable 'Rohit 2.0' ready to dominate the IPL. Kumble highlighted Rohit's effortless mastery over quality bowlers, suggesting this revitalised form will pose a significant threat to all opposing teams. He also critiqued KKR's spin strategy, particularly their delayed use of Sunil Narine, which proved costly.

title: Operations normalise in many factories as commercial LPG supplies improve
content: Liquefied petroleum gas is extensively used across industries such as automobiles and electronics, particularly in processes like brazing and paint shop operations, as well as in segments like food processing.

title: From IIT failure to Forbes list: Rise of Alakh Pandey
content: Alakh Pandey, a teacher from Prayagraj, transformed his YouTube channel into the edtech giant PhysicsWallah, making quality education accessible and affordable for millions. His journey from free online lessons to a billion-dollar valuation highlights a unique path to success in the competitive Indian education market.

title: Watch: Video reveals mid-air collision between American Airlines jet & Black Hawk helicopter
content: New footage reveals the exact moment an American Airlines jet collided with a US Army Black Hawk helicopter near Ronald Reagan Washington National Airport. The tragic January 29th crash resulted in 67 fatalities. In response, the FAA implemented safety changes, including restricting helicopter traffic and overhauling the air traffic control system.

title: PSL hotel security breach! 'Guests' till 1:25 am; player says 'Afridi not to be blamed'
content: Lahore Qalandars captain Shaheen Afridi and player Sikandar Raza are accused of breaching hotel security protocols by hosting four guests in Raza's room until 1:25 a.m., despite repeated denials from security. Raza claims it was his request to meet family and friends, stating he and Afridi were unaware of updated SOPs.

title: In Census, 'stable' live-in couple to be counted as married
content: Census 2027 will recognize live-in couples in stable unions as married.  The self-enumeration portal clarifies that such relationships should be treated as married. This marks a public acknowledgment, though officials note similar declarations were accepted in past censuses.  The question is part of the houselisting operations.

title: Pakistan a base for major terror groups, some active since 1980s: US Congress report
content: A US Congressional report highlights Pakistan as a base for numerous armed and terrorist organizations, some active since the 1980s. Despite military operations, many designated groups continue to operate from its soil, reinforcing concerns about Pakistan's links to terrorism. This has led to recent escalations and subsequent de-escalation efforts between India and Pakistan.

title: Congress flags Registrar General’s remarks, says no reason for “bulldozing” Constitution amendment bills
content: Census Commissioner Mritunjay Kumar Narayan urges people to give accurate information to Census enumerators.

title: NDA, Opposition spar in LS over efforts to free country of Maoism
content: While Shiv Sena pointed out that the red corridor has shrunk significantly due to the policies of the NDA govt and the previous UPA regime was helpless, the Congress hit back saying it was the UPA that launched Operation Green Hunt and had created the Commando Battalion for Resolute Action (CoBRA) to deal with Maoist threat; Union Home Minister Amit Shah had announced that Maoist menace would be completely eliminated by March 31, 2026

title: Kerala polls: On SDPI votes, CPI’s Binoy Viswam says party hasn’t explicitly rejected any and ‘all votes welcome’

content: According to officials, firing broke out during an argument between people travelling in two vehicles, and a stray bullet hit the Brigadier while he was out for a walk

content: The fish farmers suspect favouritism, but officials say the oversight was due to the process being conducted by a different department

content: Chelluri Narayana Rao alias Suresh was involved in killing of MLA Kidari Sarveswara Rao and CI Muddada Gandhi, says DGP Harish Kumar Gupta

content: Piloting the bill in the Lok Sabha, the Finance Minister said that 12 amendments are being made in the Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code (IBC), which came into force in 2016, that would help maximise the value for stakeholders and improve the governing process itself

title: Sonia Gandhi shows 'excellent progress', discharge expected soon, say doctors
content: Sonia Gandhi, admitted to Sir Ganga Ram Hospital on March 24, shows excellent health improvement; doctors anticipate her discharge soon after responding well to treatment

title: T. Rajhentherr moves Madras HC alleging copyright infringement of songs composed for Uyir Ulla Varai Usha
content: He had written the script and screenplay, directed, acted, composed songs, and penned the lyrics, too, for the 1983 blockbuster movie

title: Kerala Assembly Elections 2026: Poll promises may hit the coffers hard
content: While campaigning, LDF is highlighting welfare measures such as increased pensions and monthly wages implemented by the government. UDF has promised five basic ‘welfare guarantees,’ including raising welfare pensions to ₹3,000 if it comes to power. Experts question the rationale of such promises, given the huge cost involved in implementing and sustaining them

title: GBA budget: How Bengaluru’s new corporations differ in what they earn and spend
content: Property tax contributes 51% of its own source revenue in East and 42% in Central, but drops to around 34–37% in North, West and South

content: Narendra Modi said the release of the coin would honour the memory of the late Kalaignar Karunanidhi as well as the ideals he stood for

content: With 9000 schools in 20 countries hosting IKFF in 2019, the film festival aspires to use Film Pedagogy to present children with the magic of international films, using it as a means of education

content: He says the media pampers the Indian Union Muslim League and the Congress and trivialises perverse actions by their leaders

title: Minister for Home directs police to install CC cameras in public places
content: Minister also directed the police to maintain records properly and use technology at optimum level in handling difficult cases

title: Will electors from Kerala and Puducherry residing in Tamil Nadu get a holiday to cast their vote? T.N. govt. issues advisory
content: Employers have been requested to grant a paid holiday on April 9, 2026, to employees residing in Tamil Nadu who are registered voters in Kerala or Puducherry, so they can participate in their respective elections

title: IndiGo launches Shanghai-Kolkata direct flight
content: India and China resumed flights between different cities of the two countries in October last year after a five-year freeze in relations

title: Indian among two killed in UAE by debris of intercepted missiles
content: An Indian and a Pakistani were killed in Abu Dhabi by missile debris; the Indian Embassy offers condolences and support

title: Indian killed in Iranian atack on Kuwait desalination plant
content: Kuwait’s Ministry of Electricity, Water and Renewable Energy said a service building at one of its power and water distillation plants was damaged during the attack, according to the state-run KUNA news agency

title: Bengaluru Central City Corporation’s maiden ₹3,426.60 crore budget to focus on road infra, healthcare expansion
content: A major highlight is the push to strengthen public healthcare, including the introduction of a Bike Ambulance – First Responder Unit scheme to provide immediate, free life-saving care during the critical ‘golden hour’

content: A collection of interviews by ‘The Hindu’ with key candidates and political leaders as part of Kerala Assembly elections 2026 coverage

title: Watch: IPL 2026: Vidyut Sivaramakrishnan Breaks Down RCB & SRH Squads | The Hindu IPL Show 2026
content: The opening match of the 2026 Indian Premier League is between Royal Challengers Bengaluru and Sunrisers Hyderabad, takes place at M. Chinnaswamy Stadium in Bengaluru on Saturday, March 28, 2026.

title: Kerala Assembly elections 2026 | Constituency watch
content: A collection of stories on some of the key constituencies in Kerala that are set to elect their representatives to Kerala Legislative Assembly in 2026

title: 895 cases booked in week-long crackdown on drunk driving and speeding in Bengaluru
content: Authorities have warned that such special drives will continue in the coming weeks as part of a sustained effort to ensure safer roads in Bengaluru

title: Family tragedy in Anekal: Debt angle suspected in double murder, suicide attempt
content: The initial probe indicated that the man, who allegedly killed his mother and sister, was facing a mounting debt and pressure from creditors

title: Tamil Nadu Assembly election: DMK’s welfare schemes are social investments, says Kanimozhi
content: Explaining various aspects of the DMK manifesto, she said when people have money, it automatically increases their purchasing power and stimulates the economy

title: Not all welfare spendings are ‘freebies’, some are investments in human development: Palanivel Thiaga Rajan
content: “I welcome new entrants [in politics] and new ideas. If the entrant is a hugely popular actor, that is even better ... Because the people who are least engaged in politics are the youth ... anything that increases youth engagement in politics, in my view, I consider it a good thing,” says Mr. Rajan, on actor Vijay’s electoral debut

title: Assembly elections: PM Modi to visit Chennai, Puducherry on April 3 and 4
content: Mr. Modi will arrive at the Chennai airport on the afternoon of April 3 and will then fly to Puducherry, where he will address a public meeting in the evening

title: Kerala polls: UDF’s Dharmadam candidate alleges attack attempt during campaigning
content: In Taliparamba constituency in Kannur, a campaign vehicle fitted with LED displays belonging to UDF-backed Independent candidate T.K. Govindan was allegedly attacked at Malappattam

title: Suspected LeT operative arrested in Delhi's Ghazipur: police
content: Authorities uncover ties to Pakistan’s ISI and prior terror activities as the suspect faces interrogation following his arrest for promoting anti-national sentiments in the capital

title: Government clears 29 investment proposals worth ₹7,104 cr under Electronics Component Manufacturing Scheme
content: The Ministry of Electronics and IT (Meity) Secretary, S Krishnan, said the fresh investment proposal will create as many as 14,246 new jobs in the segment

title: Lok Sabha MP submits memorandum to Meghwal, seeks separate bar council for Sikkim
content: Mr. Subba highlighted that at present, the legal fraternity of Sikkim falls under the jurisdiction of the Bar Council of Assam, Nagaland, Mizoram, Arunachal Pradesh, and Sikkim

title: NCP legislators voice support for party’s merger with NCP (SP)
content: Immediately after Ajit Pawar’s demise on January 28, senior NCP (SP) leaders had claimed that a merger was in the works before the senior leader died

title: Kerala Assembly polls: Unpredictability is the norm in Vattiyurkavu constituency

title: Sunday's Elite Eight analysis: Inside UConn's stunning win over Duke
content: freshman Braylon Mullins sinks a logo 3-pointer with less than a second to go . the Huskies advance to the final Four for the third time in four years . a flurry of 3-pointers in the final four minutes pushed them over the top .

title: What's next for Duke, Tennessee after Elite Eight: Recruits, transfer portal, more
content: the 2026 men's NCAA tournament has featured its fair share of heartbreaking losses . storied programs didn't reach the regionals such as Kansas and North Carolina . ESPN's Jeff Borzello and Myron Medcalf outline expected departures and returnees .

title: NHL playoff watch: Updated East, West wild-card-race projections
content: in the east, the Boston Bruins and Columbus Blue Jackets hold the precious wild-card positions . they are trailed most closely by the Ottawa Senators and Detroit Red Wings . the Philadelphia Flyers are also in action Sunday (7 p.m. ET, ESPN+ ).

title: What's next for Duke and Notre Dame after their Elite Eight exit?
